title: Roghni Naan

seo.title: How to Make Roghni Naan — Easy Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Roghni Naan. This easy Pakistani recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Roghni Naan — a beloved Pakistani classic

description: Roghni Naan is a rich, flavorful flatbread, a staple in Pakistani cuisine, known for its soft texture and distinctive sesame seed topping. Traditionally baked in a tandoor, this indulgent naan is often brushed with ghee or butter, making it a perfect accompaniment to hearty curries and gravies. Its slightly chewy interior and crisp exterior offer a delightful culinary experience.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the warm milk, sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5 minutes until foamy. This indicates the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour and salt. Create a well in the center. Pour in the activated yeast mixture, yogurt, beaten egg, and 2 tablespoons of ghee or melted butter. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.

instructions.title: Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until it becomes smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. Form it into a ball.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Lightly grease a clean bowl with oil. Place the dough ball in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size. (This time is not included in the total active time).

instructions.title: Shape the Naan
instructions.text: Gently punch down the risen dough and divide it into 4-6 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. On a lightly floured surface, roll out each ball into an oval or teardrop shape, about 6-8 inches long and 1/4 inch thick. Press your fingertips into the surface of each naan to create indentations.

instructions.title: Add Toppings and Cook
instructions.text: Brush the surface of each shaped naan with a little water and sprinkle generously with black sesame seeds. Preheat a tawa or heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high heat. Cook each naan for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and puffed up, with charred spots appearing. Alternatively, bake in a preheated oven at 450°F (230°C) on a baking stone or sheet for 4-6 minutes.

instructions.title: Serve Fresh
instructions.text: As soon as each naan is cooked, brush generously with extra ghee or melted butter. Serve hot with your favorite Pakistani curries, kebabs, or gravies. Enjoy the rich, soft texture!

tips: For an authentic tandoor-like finish, use a very hot cast-iron skillet or a baking stone in the oven. The high heat creates the characteristic puffed and slightly charred texture.
While ghee provides the best flavor, you can substitute with unsalted butter or a neutral oil for a slightly lighter version. Ensure your yogurt is full-fat for the best richness and softness.

Roghni Naan is best served immediately, warm and fresh. If you have leftovers, lightly sprinkle with water, cover with foil, and reheat in a warm oven or microwave for a few seconds to restore softness.
article: Roghni Naan is a beloved Pakistani Baking dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
